feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Supply Chain Management
feed.subtitle: SAP Supply Chain Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: On production order release, two storage locations stock should e considered as available stock
entry.content: In SAP Public cloud, there is requirement that Bom Components material should consider the two storage location available stock in the system. Based on that system should release the Created production order. Also, it should be applicable to mass production order release transaction T-Codes as well. Kindly suggest how the system consider 2 Storage Location available stock during checking the stock/Material availability check.

entry.title: Why can CHARG be filled in MSEG but remain empty in RESB?
entry.content:
Hello,

We have observed an inconsistency between MSEG and RESB tables regarding the batch number (CHARG).

In MSEG, the field CHARG (Batch) is filled.

However, in RESB, the CHARG field is blank.

We would like to understand:

1- Under which conditions is CHARG updated in RESB?

2- Why can CHARG be filled in MSEG but remain empty in RESB?

3- Is this standard SAP behavior?

4- Does batch determination during goods issue update only MSEG and not RESB?

5- Could you please clarify the standard logic behind this behavior?

Thank you.

entry.title: SOAMANAGER: EDI OUTBOUND connection to LOBSTER
entry.content:
Hello Experts, we have a S/4HANA on prem system 2023.02 and I try to configure LOBSTER EDI connection via SOAMANAGER but I have no example how to do it. Can anybody help me regarding this topic? 
Kind regards and thank you in advance for any hint or documentation,
Michael DuMont

entry.title: GR Cancellation/reversal issue: "Deficit of PU GR quantity xxxx DAY"
entry.content:
Hi,

When I tried to post a goods cancellation reversal (102 or 122 Movement Type), I got the error 'Deficit of PU IR quantity xxxx DAY'.

My scenario is as follows: 

Our PO has one line; we don't have stock to manage, and we have the total invoiced amount greater than the received amount. And we want to post a GR cancellation to adjust the balance. As we have the flag GR-Bsd Inv already checked, we forced the system using the transactions (OMJJ & OMBZ). We managed to bypass the control, and we reverted two lines. But the system is blocking the reversal of a new line with the new amount.

We tried with different movement type but the error message is the same: 102 & 122. 

My question is why is it not allowing me to post the goods reversal? and how to overcome this issue?

Thanks a lot for your insights and support.

MM (Materials Management)

entry.title: fg order start & end date consider production start and end date of SFG based on delivery time
entry.content:
Dear Team,

In SAP Public cloud, there is requirement that production start and end date of finished material should consider the its component SFG material start and end date, based on that only finished production /planned order start and end time must be calculated. It must consider the delivery date of sales order as well as delivery time of its component material. The date must have the detailed list of header components materials level start and end date dependency in subsequent level of BOM components.

 

There is approx. 10 levels of Header BOM. how the system can meet this requirement in SAP Public cloud.

 

Please suggest how can i meet this requirement.

entry.title: DSD Settlement
entry.content: Could anyone help me with what is DSD settlement process, and when we use this, how it works, and the entire process to configure this??

entry.title: gATP determine delivery/GI date based on shipping point calendar, not supplying plant calendar?
entry.content: We use gATP for scheduling. Delivery/Goods Issue dates are currently based on the supplying plant calendar, and our plant is closed on weekends. One shipping point at this plant operates on Saturdays; we want orders assigned to that shipping point to ship on Saturday without changing the plant calendar (only that shipping point is on a 6‑day week). Can standard APO gATP use the shipping‑point calendar for delivery/GI dates so only that shipping point can be scheduled for Saturday shipment?

entry.title: SAP TM Public Cloud: Is Round‑Trip Transportation with Empty Legs Supported?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently supporting a large SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation in which Transportation Management is a core component of daily inbound and outbound operations. I would appreciate peer guidance on a specific transportation planning scenario.

Business context

Our logistics model relies on land vehicles operating in continuous round-trip cycles:

  • An empty truck travels from Location A to Location B to collect material.
  • A full truckload then transports material from Location B to Location A, after which the cycle repeats. 

Question

From a business perspective, this should ideally be represented as a single round-trip execution. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition TM, I would therefore like to model a Freight Order with:

  • an empty leg (A → B) for collection
  • a loaded leg (B → A) for transportation 

However, this does not seem to be supported in Cloud Public.

In Private Cloud implementations, I have seen similar scenarios handled using Freight Order Building Rules to enable the planning of empty movements, together with Continuous Move optimisation to link loaded and empty stages into a single round‑trip execution.

We are operating strictly within SAP standard, using delivered Fiori applications (Transportation Cockpit, Manage Freight Orders…), and are deliberately avoiding custom developments.

Is this scenario currently outside the functional scope of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition TM, or is there a standard modelling approach recommended by SAP that I may be overlooking?

Any insights based on practical implementation experience would be highly appreciated.

Kind regards,

entry.title: In SAP Public cloud, check consistency before MRP run to validate existence of master data available
entry.content:
Dear Team,

In SAP Public Cloud, how to check consistency before MRP run of Finished goods, whether master data of semi-finished and finished are available in the system or not. And then only I want to accept the output of MRP Run. 

The following master data should be checked before acceptance of MRP output at SFG & FG. 

1. BOM is available or not?

2. Routing is Available or not?

3. Production Version is correct or not?

entry.title: Create purchase requisition for Unplanned components added in production order components post MRP R
entry.content:
Dear Team,

In MTO scenario, while R&D design team adding the components after starting production, production team come to know that new part/ Material codes is to be added in production Components where some consumption is already posted for same production order. Post MRP Run, we know purchase requisition has already been posted for existing BOM Components but there will not be aby Purchase requisition can be created for newly added BOM Component in the production order post MRP Run. 

Kindly suggest, how automatic Purchase requisition can be created for newly added components in production order for consumption which have not been not included existing BOM.

entry.title: Reason Field Not available in Partial Inspection Lot in Public CLoud
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I am trying to perform partial inspection. When I try to perform Partial inspection with Follow up function SWM_RP, it doesn't show me reason field to enter any value.

But when I assign follow up function SWM_SCP to Decision code R1, it shows up reason field because it is follow up function of scrap through cost center.

My requirement is user wants to add reason field to rejection code with follow up function SWM_RP. Is it possible through standard? Or I have to go with enhancement and add any any text field?

Note: All these requirements are for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Supply Chain

entry.title: Batch creation on material to material stock posting
entry.content:
Is it possible to create a new batch automatically on doing material to material movement. Currently 

Currently system is asking for receiving batch or copying the same batch

entry.title: Transfer standard field from erp delivery to ewm delivery
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a requirement to transfer the Unloading Point (ABLAD) from ERP to EWM for outbound deliveries.

  • Source: RESB-ABLAD
  • In ERP delivery, the field is available at header level (LIKP-ABLAD), but not found at item level (LIPS)
  • Requirement: make this information available per item in EWM
  • working on s/4 Hana 2023

Current approach:

  • Extension via EEW (item level)
  • Mapping via BAdI /SCWM/EX_ERP_MAPIN_OD_SAVEREPL (MAPIN)

Questions:

  • Is this the correct BAdI for this scenario in embedded EWM?
  • How would you handle the mapping per item, given that ABLAD is not available in LIPS?
  • Do we need an ERP enhancement to pass it at item level?

thanks for support

entry.title: WT Creation Issue in S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM – Foreground vs Background Behavior
entry.content:
Guys, I am facing an issue related to WT creation in S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM.

What I did was: I created a custom storage type, defined a Putaway Control Indicator (PCI), and maintained the Storage Type Search Sequence (STSS). I also assigned the STSS to the storage type and mapped the PCI with the STSS using the configuration node “Specify STSS in SIMG.”

Then, I assigned the PCI to the product master and posted a GR. When I tried to create a WT for putaway, the system created the WT with a standard bin for putaway (S970 – clarification zone bins).

However, when I created the WT using the “Change Inbound Delivery – Deliveries” app, the system created the WT for my custom bin.

In simple terms, foreground WT creation (via Fiori app) is working as expected, but background WT creation (via Warehouse Monitor) is not.

The same issue occurs for outbound as well. I created an activity area and assigned it to the storage type, defined a stock removal indicator and assigned it to the product master, and also maintained the storage type search sequence with the removal indicator.

This setup works in the “Manage Outbound Delivery Orders” app, but not in the Warehouse Monitor, where it shows the bin determination error mentioned below.

I hope I made my problem statement clear. Waiting for your responses

entry.title: How can SAP EWM improve yard visibility without creating extra bins or unnecessary warehouse tasks?
entry.content:
In many warehouse operations, when trucks arrive in the yard, teams often create additional storage types, yard bins, or extra warehouse tasks just to improve visibility.

However, SAP EWM already provides core visibility through:

✔️ Transportation Unit (TU) tracking
✔️ Delivery linkage
✔️ Material-level information
✔️ Door and checkpoint monitoring

This raises an important operational question:

Can yard-level delivery and TU visibility be leveraged more effectively for planning and execution without adding unnecessary process complexity?

Goal:

- Reduce excess warehouse tasks
- Avoid over-engineered yard structures
- Improve operational clarity
- Simplify yard-to-warehouse flow
- Use standard SAP EWM capabilities more strategically

Discussion Point:

Would a visibility-first approach at yard level improve efficiency better than expanding warehouse structures for control?

I’d value insights from SAP EWM professionals, architects, and project experts on balancing visibility with process simplicity.

entry.title: Looking to understand support challenges in SAP environments
entry.content:
I’m searching for support challenges in SAP environments

Interested in hearing real examples from support projects, warehouse operations, manufacturing integrations, or AMS environments.

entry.title: Multi-Stage Advanced intercompany process with External Suppliers
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We would appreciate expert input from the SAP community on a design challenge we are currently evaluating in our S/4HANA landscape.
We are evaluating SAP S/4HANA Multi-Level Advanced Intercompany (MLAIC) to support a multi-stage intercompany (buy-sell) chain in a turntable setup (multiple company codes with valuated SIT and end-to-end document flow).

While MLAIC works well for internal intercompany scenarios, we are facing design challenges when integrating an external supplier (e.g., CMO) into the process chain.

⚠️Key Design Gaps / Challenges

  • No standard integration pattern for external supplier involvement
    • Missing linkage between MLAIC document chain and external procurement processes
  • Goods movement & ownership handling
    • Unclear orchestration of GI/GR postings and ownership transfer (valuated SIT vs. externally triggered shipment events). In other words, how can we do an automatic Goods receipt and Goods Issue at Intermediate company when the delivering company has physically dispatched the goods to the receiving company.
  • Tax / Localization complexity
    • Dynamic determination of tax setup in external supplier → intercompany chain scenarios, when the selling company has VAT registration in multiple EU countries and based on the supplier's location, the tax should be determined.

❓Questions to the Community

  • Has anyone implemented MLAIC with external supplier scenarios?
  • What design approaches are used to connect external procurement flows with MLAIC orchestration?
  • How are valuated SIT, GI/GR timing, and ownership transfer handled in such hybrid setups?
  • Any best practices for managing complex VAT/tax determination in these flows?

entry.title: Adding Custom Field in LTMC Template
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement to use a UII field instead of the standard Serial Number field during inventory migration using LTMC (Material Inventory Balance object).

Could you please help with:

  • Whether it is possible to add a custom UII field into the LTMC template
  • The process involved in creating and mapping the field
  • Whether this requires LTMOM enhancement, append structures, or BAPI enhancement

Any guidance or standard approach for this requirement would be helpful.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Combined Multiple purchase Requisitions into single PR post MRP Run
entry.content:
Dear Team, 

I have activated Collective requirement in MRP4 view of master data where the requirement date is same for single Material with multiple PR.  As per requirement, the system should generate single PR POST MRP Run. Along with that lot size procedure is taken  MB in MRP 1 view. Kindly suggest how this can it be possible to clubbed the requirement into single PR.

entry.title: WM Inbound Putaway Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi, I would like to clarify a scenario in inbound putaway in public cloud:

I have defined a pallet with a capacity of 1000 kg, and I have enabled the setting “Do Not Split Putaway” during the putaway process.

However, in a situation where 3 pallets arrive at the same time, I would like to understand:

  1. How does the system handle multiple pallets when “Do Not Split Putaway” is active?
  2. What is the best way to handle this scenario properly — should we use Handling Unit (HU) Management so that each pallet is processed individually?
  3. Are there recommended configurations (e.g., HU type, storage type settings, capacity check) to ensure each pallet is put away correctly without violating the “Do Not Split Putaway” rule?

Appreciate your guidance on best practice for this setup. Thank you.
